name: The White House
subject: President Macron comes to Washington
body:
Your 1600 Daily:
| | [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=3b8b899a31b165c4bfdbe1bb0fc12f3ff7a4d0e9b3e3536d98b49fa1b8e293483e485e24d3fb4e5bece263c88f7708c24640d201b6068b87) The White House • April 25, 2018
## Driving the Day
| • | This morning, French President Emmanuel Macron travels to Capitol Hill with Vice President Mike Pence to address a joint meeting of Congress.
---|---
• | The Supreme Court will hear arguments today about President Donald J. Trump’s proclamation to limit travel from countries that pose a heightened risk from terrorism. [Learn more. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=3b8b899a31b165c418d90da84a1bc167c16eda40817f8d033ec4263036edae38ab494902bdf0d86ee425173797a33c3b15ae9060b9e049a7)
## President Macron comes to Washington
**It was a ceremony befitting America’s oldest ally**. President Trump and the First Lady welcomed the French delegation yesterday with all the decorum of an official state visit, including a 21-gun salute, military inspection, and hundreds of guests waving French and American flags across the White House South Lawn.
**“In America, I saw more than America,”** French President Macron quoted from Alexis de Tocqueville, adding that the two countries share ideals of freedom and peace. “It is together that the United States and France will defeat terrorism. . . . It is together that we will counter the proliferation of arms of mass destruction, be it in North Korea or in Iran.”
**In many ways, the alliance is stronger than ever**. France is America’s third-largest European trading partner, to the tune of $1 billion in business transactions _each day_. President Trump has worked closely with France to combat terror around the world and, most recently, confront Syrian President Bashar al-Assad’s heinous chemical weapons attack against his people.
**President Trump called it a friendship “forged in revolution”** —and one that changed the course of history. “Our two great republics are linked together by the timeless bonds of history, culture, and destiny.”
• | See [highlights from yesterday’s Arrival Ceremony](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=3b8b899a31b165c4f9c9553b641669dcafb1576ac840012f5a564df2376f00daf639b1676a81d48d1c9cb69327c3b2c8e1309cd61321201a) on the South Lawn.
---|---
• | Learn more about [how Presidents Trump and Macron are working together.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=3b8b899a31b165c4f7d1606367b446d905267f6aaecdd1c5899e6356430d6c9cef4ddade0a643f0342ef4866f913710729e67ba605705a6d) [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=3b8b899a31b165c418d90da84a1bc167c16eda40817f8d033ec4263036edae38ab494902bdf0d86ee425173797a33c3b15ae9060b9e049a7)
• | Watch [President Trump’s full speech](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=3b8b899a31b165c44f698bb5c77a84a21f7d970d864537f61a75e13a119338b8a18fe38d90c1ca8825de24f70281103ca8b3d154593c00c1) welcoming the French delegation.

---
|

## A call 220 million miles away
**“ISS, this is headquarters. How do you hear us?”** The video call to the International Space Station came moments after Vice President Pence swore in Jim Bridenstine to be the next Administrator of NASA. “Mr. Vice President, Mr. Bridenstine, welcome onboard the International Space Station,” the crew replied.
**“A year ago, President Trump relaunched the National Space Council,”** the Vice President said. “The President has signed his first space policy directive that we’re going to renew America’s commitment to human space exploration, starting with the moon on to Mars.” He added that President Trump wants to make NASA’s extraordinary work for the past six decades “just a preamble” to renewed American leadership in space.
[Watch Vice President Pence talk to U.S. astronauts in space.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=3b8b899a31b165c4c1af12dd366cc4f812ab02034e9802644838a426e9f876129b88bbc50c854848e26a1dcf4641f52610c76ce20be38bec) (Clip begins at the 18:35 mark).
**More** : [President Trump’s America First National Space Strategy ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=3b8b899a31b165c405334a2377c0a8c48f9196a376dc77ee731d010e0d0024c337e7287a550c1197c9df413c2aaa469b1a8592b8b02639fe)

---
|

## Photo of the Day
Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks
**President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump host President Emmanuel Macron and Mrs. Brigitte Macron of France for the first official state visit of the Trump Administration** | April 24, 2018

[Privacy Policy](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=3b8b899a31b165c4dbfa237c786c42a52a7bd54f0d03da29b42d0ff032aa0ed7bd0ab289a141f054b0cc884ab7e5c95170b1532a530c3619) | [Contact the White House](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=3b8b899a31b165c4f3dc2bc43d12f9f9ba3cdba73be6dafb88433051eba6e502f9d10e909649bc087c87dd720606bd8a112393e5da88b5d8) | [Unsubscribe](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d46f56a2238c7a170e7a8a529b94d4a4a6db2904a5f71625c751498a40e49b28c5f5921bb5dd984a64a2e6152ddd482d65d13e2997332f79f1)

Hello --

I'm Randy Bryce, the U.S. Army veteran, cancer survivor, and union ironworker running to replace Paul Ryan in Wisconsin. You may know me as @IronStache on Twitter.

And I have to say, while I'll always be a mustache guy, Governor Tom Wolf's beard is pretty solid -- and I respect his record as a business owner who stood with workers even more.

As a union ironworker who works to provide for my family in a state run by Governor Scott Walker and the Koch brothers, I can tell you firsthand how important it is to have a governor like Tom who fights for unions and working families. In Wisconsin, we've had union rights stripped away and our public schools decimated to pay for tax cuts for the rich and huge corporations.

Wisconsin can't afford another Paul Ryan in Congress, and Pennsylvania can't afford to see Governor Tom Wolf replaced by a Trump-loving, Scott Walker-style Republican. It's essential for our future that Democrats win in both fights, but Tom and I need your help.

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Politicizing the census
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Stop Trump from playing politics with the 2020 census. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79a3a/2f5ea88d/723e6110/2cbca5f4/1395189430/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/hands-off-the-census?utm_source=ema_2018-04-24_census-petition&source=ema_2018-04-24_census-petition&utm_campaign=census-petition&utm_medium=ema") Friend, No ifs, ands, or buts about it, this is an attack on our democracy: The Trump administration is playing politics with the 2020 census. A census every 10 years is mandated in the Constitution, which states it must count "_the whole number of persons in each State._ " It determines the number of congressional districts distributed to each state, and it makes sure they receive their fair share of federal funding for everything from healthcare and education to transportation and foster care. _For the past 60 years, the census has never asked about a person's citizenship status._ But now the Trump administration plans to suppress participation by asking about citizenship. [**Tell the Trump administration not to change the census to depress participation from communities across America. Add your name here.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79a3a/2f5ea88d/723e6110/2cbca5f4/1395189430/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/hands-off-the-census?utm_source=ema_2018-04-24_census-petition&source=ema_2018-04-24_census-petition&utm_campaign=census-petition&utm_medium=ema") The Trump administration wants to use rigged census results as the excuse to gut federal funding for roads, public schools, hospitals, and local investment projects in minority communities and Democratic-leaning states. States that are actually growing could lose congressional districts, keeping outdated district lines as the status quo for another decade. An accurate census is of critical importance to me because it's a core tenant of our representative democracy. And because Washington could lose millions of dollars in federal funding if the Trump administration gets its way. That's why we're one of several states suing the feds over the citizenship question. We need to ensure the 2020 census delivers an accurate account. The Trump administration's manipulation of the 2020 census to gain political advantage is unacceptable -- and unconstitutional. [**Demand the Trump administration drop their plans to rig the 2020 census. Sign on here.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79a3a/2f5ea88d/723e6110/2cbca5f4/1395189430/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/hands-off-the-census?utm_source=ema_2018-04-24_census-petition&source=ema_2018-04-24_census-petition&utm_campaign=census-petition&utm_medium=ema") Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79a3a/2f5ea88d/723e6110/2cbca5f4/1395189430/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/hands-off-the-census?utm_source=ema_2018-04-24_census-petition&source=ema_2018-04-24_census-petition&utm_campaign=census-petition&utm_medium=ema")

subject: Introductions: Friends, meet Sherrod Brown
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
_This is our "Introductions" series. We ask you to support a lot of strong Democrats, and we want to make sure you know who they are and why they have Team Maggie's endorsement. We hope you enjoy getting to know them._
You may recognize the name Sherrod Brown.
Since his first day on Capitol Hill in 2007, Sherrod has been an independent voice for hard-working families.
In his early years in Washington, he created innovative new programs to encourage local food production and cut back on the wild swings in the market for farmers -- and saved taxpayers' money in the process.
As a member of the Banking Committee, Sherrod was an early proponent for addressing the housing crisis that caused meltdown in the credit markets. Now as the senior Democrat on the committee, he is leading the fight to focus on Main Street and manufacturing, not just Wall Street and finance.
Born and raised in Ohio, where he attended local public schools and became an Eagle Scout, Sherrod is a champion for the middle class and firmly believes that everyone deserves a chance to get ahead and stay ahead.
[**Sherrod has our endorsement, and if you 'd like to join us in supporting his reelection campaign, you can click here to donate $3 or more.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf69/2a380d31/b4572d6/f18e7e/1793089217/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAxOF9td2g0c2IiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE4MDQyNV9JbnRyby1Ccm93bl9hY3QiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2018_mwh4sb?refcode=e20180425_Intro-Brown_act&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")

| | | **Announcing ... Team Donnelly's Record Breaking 24-Hour Goal!
Goal: $10,000
Deadline: MIDNIGHT
[Pitch in $10 >>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c20cf/7377f838/3e964453/51f95fb3/3651026729/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-onedaygoal319?refcode=20180425.2nd&amount=10")**
---

Peter,

We know we don't have to tell you how much is at stake in the 2018 election. Grassroots donors have stepped up in a big way to fuel recent Democratic victories in districts that the political pundits said we could _never_ compete in. We don't know about you, but those wins in places like Alabama, Wisconsin, and Pennsylvania fired us up!

**Now, we 've been challenged to break our own online fundraising record. In 2012, after Richard Mourdock's now infamous debate, Team Donnelly's grassroots donors raised more than $8,500 in less than 24 hours! We know it'll be tough, but we think if everyone chips in today, we can break this record. Will you help us out?**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

Breaking this 24-hour record will prove to wealthy donors that Indiana's Senate seat isn't for sale to the highest bidder. It will prove that our team is ready for the fight in November -- and, most importantly, it will send a message that Democrats can compete, and win, _anywhere_ in 2018.

We have to re-elect Joe, and it won't happen unless we build a campaign that can reach voters in each of Indiana's 92 counties. We're not leaving any county behind. **If there 's been one lesson we've learned this year, it's that we have to compete everywhere.**

Peter, the Koch brothers and Mitch McConnell's allies are already working overtime to smear Joe's record on TV. We're ready to show them the power of our team and smash this record to pieces.

name: Roy Cooper
subject: This isn't good enough
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1203494/2fde7c66/4629ee9f/1569cdf8/2317200115/VEsH/ "https://cooper.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/support-our-teachers?gpsid=29_43_74&source=em18_180425_edupay_adv_gq_ful__&utm_campaign=em18_180425_edupay_adv_gq_ful__&utm_medium=email&utm_source=rcoop&utm_content=1")
---
Hello,

The new teacher pay rankings are out -- and they aren't good enough for North Carolina. We come in at 37th in the country in average teacher pay.

That is unacceptable.

Our teachers are working second jobs and some of our best are leaving our state and even the profession because of a lack of pay and respect from Republican legislative leaders.

Last year I proposed a budget that would get teacher pay to at least the national average in 5 years. But Republican leaders only passed smaller raises and left most veteran teachers out altogether.

[**Enough is enough. Add your name here to join me in calling on Republicans in the legislature to fund more teacher pay raises.**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1203494/2fde7c66/4629ee9f/1569cdfb/2317200115/VEsE/ "https://cooper.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/support-our-teachers?gpsid=29_43_74&source=em18_180425_edupay_adv_gq_ful__&utm_campaign=em18_180425_edupay_adv_gq_ful__&utm_medium=email&utm_source=rcoop&utm_content=2+-+Enough+is+enough+Add+your+name+here+to+j")

In 2014, Republican legislative leaders claimed that their teacher pay plan would move North Carolina to 32nd nationally for teacher pay.

That did not happen. And they continue to fall far short when it comes to giving teachers decent raises.

As governor, I spend a lot of time in our public schools. I know how hard our teachers are working. I know how much they care. I see it in every classroom I visit.

They deserve better than this.

[**Add your name here.**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1203494/2fde7c66/4629ee9f/1569cdfa/2317200115/VEsF/ "https://cooper.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/support-our-teachers?gpsid=29_43_74&source=em18_180425_edupay_adv_gq_ful__&utm_campaign=em18_180425_edupay_adv_gq_ful__&utm_medium=email&utm_source=rcoop&utm_content=3+-+Add+your+name+here")

Thanks for your support on this crucial issue,

Roy | | [**Add your name ->**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1203494/2fde7c66/4629ee9f/1569cdfd/2317200115/VEsC/ "https://cooper.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/support-our-teachers?gpsid=29_43_74&source=em18_180425_edupay_adv_gq_ful__&utm_campaign=em18_180425_edupay_adv_gq_ful__&utm_medium=email&utm_source=rcoop&utm_content=4+-+Add+your+name+rarr")
